defmodule WordCount.MixProject do
  use Mix.Project

  def project do
    [
      app: :wordcount,
      version: "0.1.0",
      elixir: "~> 1.14",
      start_permanent: Mix.env() == :prod,
      escript: escript(),
      deps: deps()
    ]
  end

  def application do
    [
      extra_applications: [:logger]
    ]
  end

  defp escript do
    [main_module: WordCount.CLI]
  end

  defp deps do
    []
  end
end
EOF

cat > /workspace/lib/wordcount.ex <<'EOF'
defmodule WordCount do
  @moduledoc """
  Counts words in text.

  A word is a contiguous sequence of Unicode letters or digits. Hyphenated
  compounds are counted as one word.
  """

  @word_regex ~r/\p{L}+(?:-\p{L}+)?|\p{N}+/u

  def count_words(text) when is_binary(text) do
    text
    |> Regex.scan(@word_regex)
    |> length()
  end

  def count_file(path) when is_binary(path) do
    path
    |> File.read()
    |> case do
      {:ok, contents} -> {:ok, count_words(contents)}
      {:error, reason} -> {:error, reason}
    end
  end
end
EOF

cat > /workspace/lib/wordcount/cli.ex <<'EOF'
defmodule WordCount.CLI do
  @moduledoc """
  Command-line interface for counting words in a file.
  """

  def main(args) do
    args
    |> parse_args()
    |> run()
  end

  def parse_args(args) do
    case args do
      ["--help"] -> {:help, nil}
      ["-h"] -> {:help, nil}
      [] -> {:help, nil}
      [path] -> {:file, path}
      _ -> {:error, "usage: wordcount <file>\n       wordcount --help"}
    end
  end

  def run({:help, _}) do
    IO.puts("""
    Usage: wordcount <file>

    Counts the words in the given file and prints the count.
    """)

    0
  end

  def run({:file, path}) do
    case WordCount.count_file(path) do
      {:ok, count} ->
        IO.puts(count)
        0

      {:error, reason} ->
        IO.puts(:stderr, "error: #{:file.format_error(reason)}")
        1
    end
  end

    IO.puts(:stder...

defmodule WordCount.CLI do
  @moduledoc """
  Command-line interface for counting words in a file.
  """

  def main(args) do
    args
    |> parse_args()
    |> run()
  end

  def parse_args(args) do
    case args do
      ["--help"] -> {:help, nil}
      ["-h"] -> {:help, nil}
      [] -> {:help, nil}
      [path] -> {:file, path}
      _ -> {:error, "usage: wordcount <file>\n       wordcount --help"}
    end
  end

  def run({:help, _}) do
    IO.puts("""
    Usage: wordcount <file>

    Counts the words in the given file and prints the count.
    """)

    0
  end

  def run({:file, path}) do
    case WordCount.count_file(path) do
      {:ok, count} ->
        IO.puts(count)
        0

      {:error, reason} ->
        IO.puts(:stderr, "error: #{:file.format_error(reason)}")
        1
    end
  end

  def run({:error, message}) do
    IO.puts(:stderr, message)
    1
  end
end
EOF

defmodule WordCount.MixProject do
  use Mix.Project

  def project do
    [
      app: :workspace,
      version: "0.1.0",
      elixir: "~> 1.14",
      start_permanent: Mix.env() == :prod,
      deps: deps()
    ]
  end

  # Run "mix help compile.app" to learn about applications.
  def application do
    [
      extra_applications: [:logger]
    ]
  end

  # Run "mix help deps" to learn about dependencies.
  defp deps do
    [
      # {:dep_from_hexpm, "~> 0.3.0"},
      # {:dep_from_git, git: "https://github.com/elixir-lang/my_dep.git", tag: "0.1.0"}
    ]
  end
end

defmodule WordCount do
  @moduledoc """
  Documentation for `WordCount`.
  """

  @doc """
  Hello world.

  ## Examples

      iex> WordCount.hello()
      :world

  """
  def hello do
    :world
  end
end

defmodule WordCountTest do
  use ExUnit.Case
  doctest WordCount

  test "greets the world" do
    assert WordCount.hello() == :world
  end
end

benchmarks.objective: Write a CLI that counts words in a file and passes its own tests.

steps.summary:
Operational target:
- STATUS must be running.
- RESTART_REQUIRED must be false.
- health.json must report service orchid-demo on port 8080.
- run.log must include a restart entry.

args.command: cd /workspace && ls -la ops && id && stat -c '%A %U %G %n' ops ops/service.env ops/README.md

args.command: cd /workspace && printf 'test' > ops/testwrite.tmp; echo write_status=$?; ls -l ops/testwrite.tmp; rm -f ops/testwrite.tmp

cd /workspace && cat > ops/service.env <<'EOF'
SERVICE=orchid-demo
STATUS=running
PORT=8080
RESTART_REQUIRED=false
EOF
cat > ops/health.json <<'EOF'
{
  "service": "orchid-demo",
  "status": "running",
  "port": 8080
}
EOF
printf '%s restarted orchid-demo on port 8080\n' "$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)" >> ops/run.log
cat ops/service.env
printf '\n--- health.json ---\n'
cat ops/health.json
printf '\n--- run.log ---\n'
cat ops/run.log

args.command:
cd /workspace &&
grep -q '^STATUS=running$' ops/service.env &&
grep -q '^RESTART_REQUIRED=false$' ops/service.env &&
test -f ops/health.json &&
grep -Eq '"service"[[:space:]]*:[[:space:]]*"orchid-demo"' ops/health.json &&
grep -Eq '"status"[[:space:]]*:[[:space:]]*"running"' ops/health.json &&
grep -Eq '"port"[[:space:]]*:[[:space:]]*8080' ops/health.json &&
grep -qi 'restarted orchid-demo' ops/run.log &&
echo ACCEPTANCE_CHECK_PASSED

args.command:
cd /workspace && cat > ops/service.env <<'EOF'
SERVICE=orchid-demo
STATUS=running
PORT=8080
RESTART_REQUIRED=false
EOF
cat > ops/health.json <<'EOF'
{
  "service": "orchid-demo",
  "status": "running",
  "port": 8080
}
EOF
printf '%s\n' "$(date -u '+%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ') restarted orchid-demo" >> ops/run.log

benchmarks.objective:
Bring the seeded service state online. Update `ops/service.env`, write a health
document at `ops/health.json`, and append a restart entry to `ops/run.log`.

success_check.command:
cd /workspace &&
grep -q '^STATUS=running$' ops/service.env &&
grep -q '^RESTART_REQUIRED=false$' ops/service.env &&
test -f ops/health.json &&
grep -Eq '"service"[[:space:]]*:[[:space:]]*"orchid-demo"' ops/health.json &&
grep -Eq '"status"[[:space:]]*:[[:space:]]*"running"' ops/health.json &&
grep -Eq '"port"[[:space:]]*:[[:space:]]*8080' ops/health.json &&
grep -qi 'restarted orchid-demo' ops/run.log

steps.summary:
Recovery task: fix app/healthcheck.sh. The deterministic check intentionally
fails before the agent repairs the seeded broken script.

args.new_string:
#!/bin/sh

status_file="app/status.txt"

if [ ! -f "$status_file" ]; then
    exit 1
fi

state=""
port=""

while IFS='=' read -r key value; do
    case "$key" in
        state) state="$value" ;;
        port) port="$value" ;;
    esac
done < "$status_file"

if [ "$state" = "ok" ] && [ "$port" = "8080" ]; then
    echo "HEALTHY state=ok port=8080"
    exit 0
fi

exit 1

args.new_string:
#!/bin/sh
status_file="$(CDPATH= cd -- "$(dirname -- "$0")" && pwd)/status.txt"
state=$(sed -n 's/^state=//p' "$status_file")
port=$(sed -n 's/^port=//p' "$status_file")

if [ "$state" = "ok" ] && [ -n "$port" ]; then
  echo "HEALTHY state=$state port=$port"
  exit 0
fi

echo "UNHEALTHY state=$state port=$port"
exit 1

status_file="app/status.txt"

if [ ! -f "$status_file" ]; then
  echo "UNHEALTHY status_file=missing"

fi

state=$(sed -n 's/^state=//p' "$status_file" | head -n 1)
port=$(sed -n 's/^port=//p' "$status_file" | head -n 1)

if [ "$state" = "ok" ] && [ "$port" = "8080" ]; then
  echo "HEALTHY state=ok port=8080"
  exit 0
fi

echo "UNHEALTHY state=$state port=$port"
exit 1
